mechanism: Broad kinase inhibition including activity against some FLT3 variants
additional_notes: Approved for CML/ALL; explored preclinically in AML.

mechanism: BCL-2 blockade combined with FLT3 inhibition to prime apoptosis and block proliferative signaling
additional_notes: Combination rationale from literature; synergy often explored in AML (not a single approved molecular entity).

mechanism: BTK inhibition and off-target effects that can modulate survival/microenvironment signaling
additional_notes: Approved for B-cell malignancies; reported off-target activity reducing AML cell survival in some preclinical studies.

mechanism: Inhibits CDKs (notably CDK9) leading to downregulation of short-lived prosurvival proteins such as MCL-1
additional_notes: Investigational; active in AML models via transcriptional suppression of survival factors.

mechanism: CDK9/CDK7 inhibition affecting transcription and lowering MCL-1 and other short-lived survival proteins
additional_notes: Also known as flavopiridol; investigational with AML trial/combinational data.

mechanism: Inhibits CDK4/6 to induce G1 cell-cycle arrest and limit proliferation
additional_notes: Approved for breast cancer; AML efficacy context-dependent and often considered in combinations.

mechanism: Inhibits the proteasome, leading to accumulation of misfolded proteins and induction of apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for multiple myeloma; tested in AML preclinical and combination studies.

mechanism: Irreversible proteasome inhibition causing proteotoxic stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved proteasome inhibitor with different toxicity/profile versus bortezomib; has preclinical AML data.

mechanism: Inhibits mTOR signaling to reduce growth and survival pathway activity
additional_notes: Also represented by temsirolimus in the text; mTOR pathway inhibition explored in AML combinations.

mechanism: HSP90 inhibition destabilizes multiple oncogenic client proteins (including FLT3), promoting degradation and loss of signaling
additional_notes: Representative of HSP90 inhibitors (also 17-AAG); investigational with preclinical activity in FLT3-driven AML.

mechanism: Inhibits BET bromodomains to suppress transcriptional programs (e.g., MYC) that drive proliferation
additional_notes: OTX015/MK-8628 family cited as examples; investigational BET inhibitors show anti-proliferative effects in AML models.

mechanism: HMG-CoA reductase inhibition with pleiotropic effects including reported cytotoxicity and synergy with other agents
additional_notes: Representing statins (text example simvastatin); epidemiologic and preclinical reports suggest AML activity or synergy.

mechanism: Microtubule targeting and other off-target effects that can impair cancer cell viability
additional_notes: Repurposing reports indicate activity against various cancer cell types including some leukemia models.
